description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who just received some raw text data that needs cleaning before it can be used for training.  
    Everything you have to touch lives under /home/user/data.  
    The starting situation is as follows (all paths are absolute):

    /home/user/data/staging/readme.tmp          → contains the single line:              temporary readme
    /home/user/data/staging/notes.tmp           → contains the single line:              some notes
    /home/user/data/staging/empty.tmp           → an **empty (0-byte)** file
    /home/user/data/staging/sample1.txt         → contains exactly:                     sample1-data
    /home/user/data/staging/sample2.txt         → contains exactly:                     sample2-data
    /home/user/data/staging/ignore.log          → contains:                             keep me
    /home/user/data/staging/nested/old.tmp      → contains:                             old tmp
    /home/user/data/staging/nested/blank.tmp    → an **empty (0-byte)** file
    /home/user/data/staging/nested/data.txt     → contains:                             nested-sample

    Goal
    ----
    1. Remove every file whose name ends in “.tmp” that is located anywhere beneath /home/user/data/staging.  
       (A total of five *.tmp files exist at the start.)

    2. Move – **not copy** – all “.txt” files found anywhere under /home/user/data/staging to a new directory  
       /home/user/data/final  (create the directory if it does not yet exist).  
       There are three such files at the start.  
       Do **not** move ignore.log or any other file types.

    3. Create a compressed archive (gzip-compressed tarball) called  
       /home/user/data/final/text_dataset.tar.gz  
       that contains only the three moved “.txt” files (flat layout inside the archive is fine).

    4. After finishing the two batch operations above, generate the summary file  
       /home/user/data/processing_report.log  with **exactly** the three lines below (no extra spaces, no blank lines):

       TMP_DELETED=5
       TXT_MOVED=3
       TXT_TOTAL_BYTES=37

       Explanations:  
       • TMP_DELETED – total *.tmp files deleted in step 1 (must be 5).  
       • TXT_MOVED  – total *.txt files moved in step 2 (must be 3).  
       • TXT_TOTAL_BYTES – combined byte size of the **contents** of the three .txt files **before** compression  
         (sample1-data = 12 B, sample2-data = 12 B, nested-sample = 13 B, for a total of 37 B).

    Constraints & Hints
    -------------------
    • Use “find … -name … -print0 | xargs -0 …” (or another safe variation) so that your commands tolerate spaces, newlines, etc.  
    • Make sure you really *move* the *.txt files − after the task finishes nothing with “.txt” should remain anywhere under /home/user/data/staging.  
    • Ignore the *.log file; it must remain untouched.  
    • The archive must be present at /home/user/data/final/text_dataset.tar.gz when you are done, but its internal directory structure is not tested.  
    • The numbers in processing_report.log must match the reality of the operations you performed; they are what the automated checker will validate.

    At the end, the directory tree should look like (illustrative):

      /home/user/data
      ├── final
      │   ├── sample1.txt
      │   ├── sample2.txt
      │   ├── data.txt
      │   └── text_dataset.tar.gz
      ├── processing_report.log
      └── staging
          ├── ignore.log
          └── (now empty of *.tmp and *.txt files)

    Perform all actions via the Linux terminal.  Do **not** manually edit the files with a text editor; rely on command-line tools, primarily “find” and “xargs”.
truth:
Initial files (with full paths and exact contents):

    /home/user/data/staging/readme.tmp          -> "temporary readme\n"
    /home/user/data/staging/notes.tmp           -> "some notes\n"
    /home/user/data/staging/empty.tmp           -> ""  (zero bytes)
    /home/user/data/staging/sample1.txt         -> "sample1-data\n"
    /home/user/data/staging/sample2.txt         -> "sample2-data\n"
    /home/user/data/staging/ignore.log          -> "keep me\n"
    /home/user/data/staging/nested/old.tmp      -> "old tmp\n"
    /home/user/data/staging/nested/blank.tmp    -> ""  (zero bytes)
    /home/user/data/staging/nested/data.txt     -> "nested-sample\n"

    Expected end-state verification points:

    1. All five *.tmp files listed above must be absent.
    2. ignore.log must still exist and be unchanged.
    3. The three *.txt files must reside under /home/user/data/final (base filenames preserved) and no *.txt may remain in /home/user/data/staging.
    4. /home/user/data/final/text_dataset.tar.gz must exist and, once extracted, contain exactly:
           sample1.txt
           sample2.txt
           data.txt
    5. /home/user/data/processing_report.log must exist and contain exactly:
           TMP_DELETED=5\n
           TXT_MOVED=3\n
           TXT_TOTAL_BYTES=37\n
